name: Turin (Italy)

colors.symbolism: the field of the city's coat of arms

description: A blue field bearing the city's coat of arms: a golden rampant bull (the toro) on a blue shield.
history: The bull (toro) is the canting emblem of Turin (Torino), in use since the medieval period; the city's name itself probably derives from the Celtic-Ligurian Taurini people.

features.role: Coat of arms of Turin (a rampant golden bull on blue)
features.symbolism: the bull is a canting reference to Turin's name (Torino)
